ecap.name: ECAP Basel
ecap.description: ECAP Basel offers integration-focused German courses with flexible pricing based on income. They specialize in helping newcomers to Switzerland integrate through language learning, with courses ranging from alphabetization to C1 level.

reviews.author: Maria S.
reviews.text: Great teachers and flexible scheduling. The income-based pricing made it affordable for me.

reviews.author: Ahmed K.
reviews.text: Good courses for integration. Sometimes classes are a bit crowded.

reviews.author: Elena P.
reviews.text: Excellent for beginners. The alphabetization course helped me a lot.

k5.name: K5 Basler Kurszentrum
k5.description: K5 Basler Kurszentrum is a community-focused language school offering German courses specifically designed for residents of Basel. They offer specialized courses for parents, women, and job seekers.

reviews.author: Fatima A.
reviews.text: The women-only course was perfect for me. Great teachers and supportive environment.

reviews.author: Thomas M.
reviews.text: Good intensive courses. The job search module was very practical.

reviews.author: Li W.
reviews.text: My wife loves the Deutsch fur Frauen course. The childcare option is a lifesaver.

academia.name: Academia Languages Basel
academia.description: Academia Languages is a professional language school offering structured German courses with official certificates. They focus on quality instruction with smaller class sizes and experienced teachers.

reviews.author: Michael B.
reviews.text: Excellent preparation for the B2 certificate. Teachers are very professional.

reviews.author: Sophie L.
reviews.text: Good quality courses but quite expensive. Worth it for the certificate prep.

reviews.author: Jan V.
reviews.text: Small classes and good individual attention. Recommended for serious learners.

migros.name: Migros Klubschule Basel
migros.description: Migros Klubschule is one of Switzerland's largest adult education institutions. They offer a wide range of German courses from beginner to advanced levels with modern teaching methods.

reviews.author: Anna K.
reviews.text: Good facilities and professional teachers. The online option was very convenient.

reviews.author: Peter H.
reviews.text: Reliable quality as expected from Migros. Classes can be large though.

reviews.author: Sarah M.
reviews.text: Great experience overall. The flexible scheduling helped me balance work and learning.

berlitz.name: Berlitz Basel
berlitz.description: Berlitz is an internationally recognized language school known for its immersive teaching method. They offer German courses with a focus on practical communication skills.

reviews.author: David R.
reviews.text: The Berlitz method really works. I improved my speaking skills rapidly.

reviews.author: Julia F.
reviews.text: Expensive but high quality. Great for business professionals.

reviews.author: Marco T.
reviews.text: Private lessons are worth the investment. Very personalized approach.

inlingua.name: inlingua Basel
inlingua.description: inlingua is a global language school network offering German courses with their proprietary teaching method. They focus on active speaking practice from day one.

reviews.author: Christina W.
reviews.text: Great focus on speaking from the start. Teachers are all native speakers.

reviews.author: Robert L.
reviews.text: The inlingua method helped me overcome my fear of speaking German.

reviews.author: Nina S.
reviews.text: Good for exam preparation. Professional environment.

vhs.name: Volkshochschule beider Basel
vhs.description: Volkshochschule beider Basel is a traditional adult education center offering affordable German courses for the community. They focus on accessible education for all.

reviews.author: Hans G.
reviews.text: Perfect for learning at a relaxed pace. Very affordable too.

reviews.author: Marie D.
reviews.text: Nice community atmosphere. Good for casual learners.

reviews.author: Paul K.
reviews.text: Great value for money. Teachers are friendly and patient.
